'''Brian G. McDermott''' (born 16 March 1970) is an English professional [[rugby league]] coach who is the head coach of {{rlnt|England}} at international level, and an assistant coach at the [[Newcastle Knights]] in the [[NRL]]. A former professional rugby league footballer, he won three [[Super League]] Grand Finals as a [[Bradford Bulls]] player and has won four Grand Finals as head coach of [[Leeds Rhinos]].<ref name="Statistics at rugbyleagueproject.org"/><ref name="Coach Statistics at rugbyleagueproject.org"/>

He was previously the head coach of [[Leeds Rhinos|Leeds]] in the Super League and of the [[USA national rugby league team|USA national team]].<ref>{{cite news|url=https://www.bbc.com/sport/rugby-league/33433760|title=McDermott takes on USA coach role|work=BBC Sport}}</ref> Nicknamed 'Big Mac', McDermott was a [[Great Britain national rugby league team|Great Britain]] international forward who played his entire career at club level for [[Bradford Bulls|Bradford]], winning [[Super League Grand Final]]s and [[Challenge Cup]]s with them.

He began his coaching career in 2003 as an assistant at [[Huddersfield Giants|Huddersfield]], taking his first senior coaching role with [[London Broncos#2006–2011: Harlequins Rugby League|Harlequins RL]] in 2006, before joining Leeds as head coach in 2010. McDermott coached Leeds to several major trophies including the [[Super League XVI|2011]], [[Super League XVII|2012]], [[Super League XVIII|2015]] and [[Super League XX|2017]] Super League titles, the [[2012 World Club Challenge]], and the [[2014 Challenge Cup|2014]] and [[2015 Challenge Cup]]s.

==Background==
McDermott was born in [[Wakefield]], [[West Riding of Yorkshire]], England. He played amateur rugby league as a junior for Eastmoor RLFC and was a [[Royal Marine]] for five years before becoming a professional player.{{citation needed|date=December 2022}}

==Playing career==
===1990s===
McDermott joined [[Bradford Bulls|Bradford Northern]] (later Bradford Bulls) in 1994, where he stayed for 10 years. He played for Bradford at prop forward in their [[1996 Challenge Cup]] final loss to [[St Helens R.F.C.|St. Helens]].<ref>{{cite web|title=steveprescottfoundation.co.uk|url=http://www.steveprescottfoundation.co.uk/About/StevePrescottStats.aspx|work=Steve Prescott Stats|publisher=Steve Prescott Foundation|access-date=3 June 2012|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20120808052156/http://www.steveprescottfoundation.co.uk/About/StevePrescottStats.aspx|archive-date=8 August 2012|url-status=dead}}</ref> He played for [[Bradford Bulls]] from the interchange bench in the [[1999 Super League Grand Final]] which was lost to St Helens.

McDermott won a [[Cap (sport)|cap]] for England in 2001 against Wales, and won caps for Great Britain in 1996 against Fiji, and 3 in 1997 against [[Australia national rugby league team|Australia]] ([[Super League (Australia)|Super League]]).

===2000s===
McDermott played for the Bradford Bulls at {{rlp|pr}} in their [[2001 Super League Grand Final]] victory against the [[Wigan Warriors]]. As [[Super League VI]] champions, the Bradford Bulls played against [[2001 NRL season|2001 NRL]] Premiers, the [[Newcastle Knights]] in the [[2002 World Club Challenge]]. McDermott played as a prop forward in Bradford's victory. He also played for Bradford from the interchange bench in their [[2002 Super League Grand Final]] loss against St Helens. After ending his playing career in 2003, McDermott moved into coaching.{{citation needed|date=December 2022}}

==Coaching career==
===Harlequins RL===
McDermott had spells on the coaching staff at [[Super League]] clubs [[Huddersfield Giants|Huddersfield]] and [[Leeds Rhinos|Leeds]] before accepting his first top-flight head coaching role at [[London Broncos|Harlequins]] in July 2006, replacing [[Tony Rea]]. McDermott won his first match as a head coach against [[Castleford Tigers|Castleford]]. He went on to claim five wins and four losses in the remainder of 2006.

McDermott remained at Harlequins for a further four seasons but the team decreased in performance. The team which had been in the top 5 in the year before McDermott's arrival finished 7th, then 9th then 9th again. Harlequins RL went on three long streaks of very poor form in the last season and a half of McDermott's tenure.

The 2009 saw a second half of the season collapse with the team winning only 1 of its last 12 games and finishing 11th.

The 2010 season saw an early win against [[Catalans Dragons]], but again only one win in the first 11 games.  
[[File:Brian Mac.JPG|thumb|right|McDermott in charge of Harlequins Rugby League in 2010]]
There were quite a few big losses by scores such as 62–4, 58-0 and 52–12.  After a midseason streak of four wins in five games, the team again returned to losing form, losing 7 out of the last 9. McDermott had not secured the team's better players to new contracts, such as [[Louie McCarthy-Scarsbrook]], Will Sharp, Danny Orr, Oliver Wilkes and Luke Williamson and the team had finished 11th and 13th in successive seasons.{{citation needed|date=December 2022}}

===Leeds Rhinos===
McDermott left Harlequins and returned to Leeds as Assistant Manager at the end of the 2010 season. On 25 October 2010, following the resignation of incumbent coach [[Brian McClennan]], it was announced that McDermott would take over as head coach at Headingley on a three-year contract.

McDermott coached Leeds to the [[2011 Challenge Cup Final]] defeat by [[Wigan Warriors|Wigan]] at [[Wembley Stadium]].<ref>{{cite news|title=Wigan edge thrilling cup final|url=http://uk.eurosport.yahoo.com/27082011/63/wigan-edge-thrilling-cup-final.html|access-date=21 November 2019|newspaper=uk.eurosport.yahoo.com|date=27 August 2011}}</ref><ref>{{cite news|last=Scott|first=Ged|title=Wigan's Challenge Cup win a team effort - Joel Tomkins|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/14695883|access-date=21 November 2019|newspaper=[[BBC Sport]]|date=27 August 2011}}</ref><ref>{{cite news|last=skysports.com|title=Maguire salutes Wembley heroes|url=http://www.skysports.com/story/0,,12215_7131244,00.html|access-date=21 November 2017|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20170226214404/http://www.freepressseries.co.uk/uk_national_sport/9220535.Wigan_edge_thrilling_cup_final/|url-status=dead}}</ref><ref>{{cite news|title=O'Loughlin savours special cup win|url=http://uk.eurosport.yahoo.com/28082011/63/o-loughlin-savours-special-cup-win.html|access-date=21 November 2019|newspaper=uk.eurosport.yahoo.com|date=28 August 2011}}</ref><ref>{{cite news|title=Wigan's Sam Tomkins punished for gesture at Leeds fans|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/14695413|access-date=21 November 2019|newspaper=[[BBC Sport]]|date=30 August 2011}}</ref>

However, McDermott guided Leeds to [[2011 Super League Grand Final]] victory over [[St Helens R.F.C.|St. Helens]] at [[Old Trafford]],<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.theguardian.com/sport/2011/oct/08/super-league-grand-final-match-report?newsfeed=true|title=Leeds claim Grand Final glory as inspired Rob Burrow sinks St Helens|date=8 October 2011|work=The Guardian|access-date=21 November 2019}}</ref><ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.telegraph.co.uk/sport/rugbyleague/8815944/St-Helens-16-Leeds-32-match-report.html|title=St Helens 16 Leeds 32|date=8 October 2011|work=The Daily Telegraph|access-date=21 November 2019}}</ref> after finishing in 5th place in the league.  McDermott replicated the same feat in 2012, where Leeds won the World Club Challenge against [[Manly-Warringah Sea Eagles|Manly]].

He coached [[Leeds Rhinos|Leeds]] to their [[2012 Challenge Cup Final]] defeat by [[Warrington Wolves|Warrington]] at [[Wembley Stadium]].<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.theguardian.com/sport/2012/aug/25/leeds-warrington-challenge-cup |title=Warrington's battered Brett Hodgson recovers to see off Leeds in final|date=25 August 2012|work=Guardian UK|access-date=20 November 2019}}</ref><ref name="Leeds' Kevin Sinfield stars in Grand Final triumph against Warrington">{{cite web|url=https://www.theguardian.com/sport/2012/oct/06/warrington-leeds-grand-final|title=Leeds' Kevin Sinfield stars in Grand Final triumph against Warrington|work=The Guardian|date=6 October 2012|access-date=20 November 2019}}</ref><ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/19362692|title=Leeds Rhinos 18–35 Warrington Wolves|date=25 August 2012|work=BBC Sport|access-date=20 November 2019}}</ref><ref>{{cite web
 |url=http://www.warringtonwolves.org/warrington-wolves-are-challenge-cup-winners-2012-5628 
 |title=Warrington Wolves Are Challenge Cup Winners 2012! 
 |date=25 August 2012 
 |work=Warrington Wolves Official Site 
 |access-date=20 November 2019
 |url-status=dead 
 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20131217230118/http://www.warringtonwolves.org/warrington-wolves-are-challenge-cup-winners-2012-5628 
 |archive-date=17 December 2013
}}</ref>

Two months later, the two sides met again in the [[2012 Super League Grand Final]] which Leeds won for the sixth time in nine years with the victory over Warrington at [[Old Trafford]]<ref>{{cite news|title=Grand Final: Warrington 18-26 Leeds|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/19805055|access-date=20 November 2019|newspaper=BBC Sport|date=6 October 2012}}</ref><ref name="Leeds' Kevin Sinfield stars in Grand Final triumph against Warrington"/> and making it two out of two for McDermott in his first two years as head coach.

By coincidence, on 12 April 2013 [[Leeds United F.C.|Leeds United]] announced their new manager as [[Brian McDermott (footballer)|Brian McDermott]], meaning that both the [[rugby league]] and [[association football|football]] teams in the city of Leeds had managers with the same name. Both sides also had players by the name of Ryan Hall at the same time, one [[Ryan Hall (rugby league)|a 25-year-old winger]] for the rugby league side, the other [[Ryan Hall (footballer)|a 25-year-old winger]] for Leeds United.

In 2014 McDermott coached Leeds in the [[2014 Challenge Cup Final]] victory over [[Castleford Tigers|Castleford]] at [[Wembley Stadium]],<ref name="Leeds lift Challenge Cup after Ryan Hall’s double stuns Castleford">{{cite web|url=https://www.theguardian.com/sport/2014/aug/23/catleford-leeds-challenge-cup-final-match-report|title=Leeds lift Challenge Cup after Ryan Hall's double stuns Castleford|work=The Guardian|date=23 August 2014|access-date=13 November 2019}}</ref> their first [[Challenge Cup]] win since 1999.  They were knocked out of the playoffs against [[Catalans Dragons]] after finishing 6th in the league, their worst position since 1996.

In 2015 McDermott coached Leeds to a historic treble, winning the [[2015 Challenge Cup|Challenge Cup]] 50–0,<ref name="Leeds emphatically shut out Hull KR to lift Challenge Cup">{{cite web|url=https://www.theguardian.com/sport/2015/aug/29/leeds-emphatically-shut-out-hull-kr-to-win-challenge-cup|title=Leeds emphatically shut out Hull KR to lift Challenge Cup|work=The Guardian|date=29 August 2015|access-date=8 November 2019}}</ref><ref>{{cite news |title=Scoreboard |newspaper=[[Rugby Leaguer & League Express]] |issue=2982 |date=31 August 2015 |page=31}}</ref><ref name=BBC2908>{{cite web| url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/34086760 |title=Challenge Cup final: Hull KR 0-50 Leeds Rhinos
 |work=BBC Sport |date=29 August 2015 |access-date=29 August 2015}}</ref> scoring a last second try against [[Huddersfield Giants|Huddersfield]] to win the [[League Leaders' Shield]], and beating [[Wigan Warriors|Wigan]] in a major final for the first time by 22–20 in the [[2015 Super League Grand Final]] victory at [[Old Trafford]].<ref name="Leeds pip Wigan to seal treble after brilliant, breathless Grand Final">{{cite web|url=https://www.theguardian.com/sport/2015/oct/10/leeds-wigan-super-league-grand-final-match-report|title=Leeds pip Wigan to seal treble after brilliant, breathless Grand Final|work=The Guardian|date=10 October 2015|access-date=8 November 2019}}</ref>

McDermott was a nominee for the [[BBC Sports Personality of the Year Coach Award]] at the end of the season.  Leeds endured a difficult season in 2016 as they were flooded out of their training ground for the first half of the season and suffered a run of injuries that left them having to fight against relegation in the Qualifiers.  At the start of 2017, with only the addition of [[Matt Parcell]] to the Leeds squad from the previous year, they finished runners up to [[Castleford Tigers|Castleford]] in the table. They then beat Castleford in the [[2017 Super League Grand Final|2017 Grand Final]] at [[Old Trafford]], with long-serving players [[Rob Burrow]] and [[Danny McGuire]] leaving the club as champions.<ref name="Castleford 6-24 Leeds: Grand Final 2017 – as it happened">{{cite web|url=https://www.theguardian.com/sport/live/2017/oct/07/castleford-v-leeds-grand-final-2017-live-super-league-rugby?page=with:block-59d9128de4b00593b4d67a05#liveblog-navigation|title=Castleford 6-24 Leeds: Grand Final 2017 – as it happened|work=The Guardian|date=7 October 2017|access-date=6 November 2019}}</ref><ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.bbc.com/sport/rugby-league/41536394|title=Grand Final 2017: Castleford 6-24 Leeds Rhinos|date=7 October 2017|work=BBC Sport|access-date=6 November 2019}}</ref><ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.theguardian.com/sport/2017/oct/07/castleford-leeds-super-league-grand-final-match-report|title=Danny McGuire guides Leeds to Grand Final success over Castleford|date=7 October 2017|work=The Guardian|access-date=6 November 2019}}</ref>

After a run of seven losses in a row, the club sacked McDermott as head coach in July 2018.

===Toronto Wolfpack===
McDermott took over at the [[Toronto Wolfpack]] ahead of the [[2019 RFL Championship season]].
[[File:Brian McDermott Toronto Wolfpack.jpg|thumb|right|McDermott in charge of Toronto in 2019]]

===Oldham RLFC===
On 5 July 2021 it was reported that he had joined [[Oldham RLFC|Oldham]] as a coaching consultant to Brendan Sheridan, following the departure of former head coach [[Matt Diskin]] in June.<ref>{{cite web|website=Love Rugby League|date=5 July 2021|accessdate=6 July 2021|title=Brian McDermott makes surprise rugby league return with Oldham|url=https://www.loverugbyleague.com/post/brian-mcdermott-makes-surprise-rugby-league-return-with-oldham/}}</ref>

===Featherstone Rovers===
It was reported on 22 October 2021 that he had taken over as head-coach from [[James Webster (rugby league)|James Webster]]<ref>{{cite news|website=Sky Sports|date=22 October 2021|accessdate=22 October 2021|title=Brian McDermott set for Featherstone Rovers after James Webster’s departure|url=https://www.skysports.com/rugby-league/news/12196/12441154/brian-mcdermott-set-for-featherstone-rovers-after-james-websters-departure}}</ref>

===Newcastle Knights===
McDermott became assistant coach at the [[Newcastle Knights]] in November 2022, ahead of the [[2023 NRL season]] in Australia.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.newcastleknights.com.au/news/2023/02/02/mcdermott-on-english-super-league-triumphs-and-joining-the-coaching-staff-maxwell/|title=McDermott on English Super League triumphs and joining the coaching staff|date=2 February 2023|website=Newcastle Knights}}</ref>

==International==
===United States===
In 2015, McDermott was named head coach of the {{rlnt|United States}}, combining the job with his role at Leeds.<ref>[https://web.archive.org/web/20150714181635/http://asia.eurosport.com/rugby-league/mcdermott-lands-usa-head-coach-role_sto4812178/story.shtml McDermott lands USA head coach role]</ref> His first outing as coach of the USA Hawks was in the [[2017 Rugby League World Cup qualification – Americas|2017 Rugby League World Cup qualifiers]], in which he guided the United States to victories over [[Jamaica national rugby league team|Jamaica]] and [[Canada national rugby league team|Canada]], and subsequently qualified for the [[2017 Rugby League World Cup|2017 World Cup]]. After qualifying for the tournament he announced he was to stay on as the US coach until at least the end of the 2017 World Cup.<ref>{{Cite web|url=http://www.asiapacificrl.com/2016/03/31/mcdermott-to-coach-usa-at-world-cup/|title=McDermott to coach USA at World Cup}}</ref>

===England===
On 20 April 2026 it was reported that he had taken up the role of head-coach for {{rlnt|England}}<ref>{{cite web|website=Love Rugby League|date=20 Apr 2026|author=Matthew Shaw|title=Brian McDermott lands England head coach role|url=https://www.loverugbyleague.com/post/brian-mcdermott-lands-england-head-coach-role}}</ref>

==Honours==

*'''[[Super League]]''' (3): 1997, 2001, 2003
*'''[[League Leaders' Shield]]''' (3): 1999, 2001, 2003
*'''[[Challenge Cup]]''' (2): 2000, 2003
*'''[[World Club Challenge]]''': 2002

===Coach===
*'''[[Super League]]''' (4): 2011, 2012, 2015, 2017
*'''[[League Leaders' Shield]]''' (1): 2015
*'''[[Challenge Cup]]''' (2): 2014, 2015
*'''[[World Club Challenge]]''': 2012
